story: The Referee Arrives (salmon bycatch meets the AI that counts the fish)

roles.sky: #f2d8a0 dawn cream to #e8a86b warm rose-amber (low morning light)
roles.ground: #cbb894 bone-tan gravel bars + #8a7a5c taupe shadow (the exposed ribcage of a low river)
roles.water: #7fae9e milky glacial jade shallows + #3f6f63 deep pool green
roles.hero: chrome silver-blue Salmon + #c23b2e sockeye crimson (spawning red)
roles.accent_act1: #c23b2e sockeye crimson (the fish is the focal signal while the argument rages)
roles.accent_act2: counter-brass #a8763e housing + dial-cream #f7f1df face with ink digits: the count signal is a PHYSICAL mechanical object (cream dial, rolling digits, click motion), not a glowing HUD color. Deliberately NOT mint/phosphor (mint was the 07-08 caribou count overlay and the 07-11 forecast line, the channel's creeping house color for 'the AI's clean number'), NOT IR citron, NOT server-teal.

palette.mood: exposed, drained, honest morning light: the river laid bare is the argument laid bare. The act-shift from crimson accent to the cream-and-brass mechanical counter IS the story arriving.
palette.divergence: 07-19 was cold indigo night + ember orange + server teal; 07-20 was black-spruce green + midnight-blue to rose-gold + IR magenta-citron + vermilion. This run is a DAY palette dominated by bone gravel + milky jade + dawn amber: no night sky, no IR, no ember. Also guarded against 07-10 (teaching-the-machine-to-see: sockeye crimson + glacial river + gravel): that board was a close-up single-fish staging in saturated river blues; this one is a wide two-bank DIORAMA in bone-tan daylight with an ensemble cast, crimson spent only as the act-1 focal accent, and the build must NOT drift back toward a close-up-on-one-fish staging.

light.key: warm amber directional from right, long cool shadows raking left across gravel bars
light.fill: cool jade bounce up from the water onto characters' underside
light.rim: pale gold rim on every hero silhouette (RimLight), stronger on the referee in the signature shot
light.depth: Stage3D planes with Atmosphere on the far ridge band; water shimmer as a separate motion region; ContactShadow under every grounded cast member; forms read dimensional via tones()/FormGradient per the depth bar

shape_language.the_argument: jagged and angular: shout-shards, opposing tilted StatCards, hard zigzag speech geometry
shape_language.the_river_and_fish: smooth S-curves, braided channel curves, salmon undulation
shape_language.the_count: MECHANICAL, not HUD: a hand-held clicker dial (rotating counter wheel, hard click settle) and an odometer-style flip-digit tally chip on the weir. NO ScanReticle, no brackets, no lock-on marks anywhere in this piece: the count is a physical object you could hold, which is the story's whole point (who HOLDS the counter)
shape_language.institutions_vs_hands: NOAA lab desk world rectilinear gray; the Sitka weir hand-built warm timber with rounded pegs (self-determination reads as craftsmanship, not machinery)
shape_language.why: the story is an angular argument laid over an organic river, resolved by precise-but-gentle count geometry: three registers a muted viewer can tell apart at a glance

casting.hero: Salmon (fauna.tsx, chrome + spawning red variants) as the through-line character; Character rig as the human ensemble (subsistence family flannel/vest, pollock crew worker, buried observer, THE REFEREE, Sitka biologist vest)
casting.supporting: FishingBoat (vehicles.tsx), Raven (recurring honest-tally gag, open-to-close match cut), Moose alert-pose cameo (tracks the ticking counter sound), RiverBG (biomes.tsx, THE salmon stage, summer), props kit (StatCard, SwingSign, Nameplate, Stamp, PenAndDocument), PaperStorm
casting.from_manifest: every cast member above is on the shelf; freshness comes from staging (dueling banks, boom-up reveal, weir authorship beat)
net_new: TallyCounter: a reusable mechanical count mark (props.tsx): hand-held clicker dial variant (rotating wheel, click settle) + odometer flip-digit chip variant (rolling cream digits in a brass housing). THE run's count signal, physically distinct from any HUD reticle; built to tones/RimLight/ContactShadow bar, digits as params
VideoWeir: a reusable camera-lane weir set piece (props.tsx) with timber legs, camera housing, and a TallyCounter chip mounted on it: the missing fisheries-monitoring prop; all copy as params
Character rig growth: 'referee' outfit (striped vest + whistle) + a raised-clicker pose, registered to the rig not one-offed
casting.count_mark_divergence: yesterday's Vale dispatch used the ScanReticle RED/citron IR thermal LOCK-ON that SNAPS around a subject. This run REMOVES the reticle entirely: the count is a MECHANICAL clicker/odometer with cream dial faces and brass housing, clicking upward, held in hands. Different object class, different color logic (physical cream/brass vs glowing HUD), different verb (tally vs lock), different ownership grammar (held, not overlaid).

motion_language.argument_beats: sharp opposing jabs and shakes, cards slamming with overshoot, stamps thudding
motion_language.fish: continuous S-curve undulation; the salmon fling gets 180-degree motion blur and a splash
motion_language.the_count: mechanical clicker spin with a hard eased settle; odometer digits roll upward and land with a click, never a glowing overlay
hero_moves: the raven decimal-snatch (fast peck + flap-off, motion blur)
the whistle anticipation (chest inflate, 8-frame hold) before the signature boom
the SIGNATURE BOOM-UP: composed Stage3D crane rising + dolly back over a true rotateX ground plane, atmosphere deepening as the empty river reveals
motion_language.earned_blur: raven snatch, salmon fling, card slams; nothing else smears

composition.staging: 9:16 master with all story inside the centered 4:5 safe box; dueling banks stage left/right of center; captions bottom-third above the 4:5 cut
composition.negative_space: the signature frame spends its emptiness on purpose: a small referee centered in a wide bone-colored river bed, the eye caught by the cream-and-brass counter and its click-driven digit flip (motion, not hue, carries the look-here)
composition.signature_shot: BOOM-UP PULL-BACK: referee ankle-deep on a dry gravel bar, clicker locked on a true count, over a river with almost no fish. HARD TARGET: at the widest frame, AT MOST 3 fish visible in the entire river: the emptiness is composed on purpose, not improvised at build time
composition.focal_rule: one accent hue per frame (crimson in act one, the cream-dial counter in act two); off-palette = look here, spent once

craft_advance: Migrate the signature scene onto Stage3D: a composed craneDown-reversed (rise) + dollyThrough-back camera move over a true rotateX ground plane (the manifest's number-one 'known next advance': Episode scenes onto stage3d). Supporting: TallyCounter + VideoWeir join props.tsx as reusable library assets and the Character rig gains the referee outfit + clicker pose.
